danger_signals.visual_form: Large vertical drops; lemming sprite position relative to ground level
danger_signals.meaning: Lemmings die if they fall from too great a height—player must visually judge safe vs. unsafe drop distances

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated liquid terrain obstacle; distinct color/animation from solid landscape
danger_signals.meaning: Contact with lava is fatal; environmental hazard that requires skill assignment or pathfinding to avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Specialized level obstacles; visual distinctness from navigable terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards that kill lemmings on contact; must be avoided or cleared

danger_signals.visual_form: Timer UI element; countdown visual feedback
danger_signals.meaning: When timer expires, all lemmings explode; world-state of failure/time pressure visualized through countdown

danger_signals.visual_form: Assigned skill visual state on lemming; 5-second timer display
danger_signals.meaning: Assigned lemming will self-destruct, destroying nearby destructible landscape but not other lemmings

danger_signals.visual_form: Vertical obstruction above lemming; spatial layout of terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Climbers cannot pass overhangs and will fall if they hit one; visual obstacle detection required

safety_signals.meaning: Safe walking surface for lemmings; primary navigation path

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated exit point in level; visual landmark distinct from obstacles
safety_signals.meaning: Lemmings that reach this location are saved; primary objective marker

safety_signals.visual_form: Lemming assigned Floater skill; visible parachute visual state
safety_signals.meaning: Safe descent from any height; negates fall damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Built 12-step structure; visual ladder formed from lemming labor
safety_signals.meaning: Safe vertical passage; allows lemmings to ascend or traverse otherwise impassable terrain

safety_signals.visual_form: Lemming assigned Blocker role; stationary positioning; visual barrier
safety_signals.meaning: Safe redirection point; other lemmings reverse direction on contact without harm

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Anthropomorphized lemming sprite; distinct visual appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Entrance trap doors above; exit goal location; path-dependent behavior changes based on assigned skills

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer UI visible and counting down; lemmings spawning and active on level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemmings follow programmed paths; respond to assigned skills; world is interactive and mutable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All lemmings explode; timer reaches zero; visual feedback of failure state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All lemmings become inactive/destroyed; level ends; player receives failure evaluation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sufficient lemmings reach exit location; visual progression marker
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rescued lemmings leave level; player advances to next difficulty tier

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Landscape changes as lemmings use Basher, Miner, or Digger skills; destructible terrain visually alters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level geometry becomes dynamic; new paths open; impassable areas become passable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lemmings make contact with lava, fire, or traps; death animation plays; lemming disappears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemming count decreases; loss state visualized through lemming removal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Skill UI shows available quantity of each skill type; limited numbers per level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must strategically ration skills; world state constrains player choices through resource scarcity

environmental_storytelling.technique: Obstacle progression and level design narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Levels grouped into four difficulty categories; obstacles increase in complexity; visual and mechanical progression tells story of escalating challenge and player mastery
Lava pools, fire, booby traps, and large drops positioned as narrative obstacles; their visual placement communicates environmental danger and world state of 'hostile terrain'

environmental_storytelling.technique: Lemming behavior communication through sprite and animation
environmental_storytelling.example: Green-haired blue-robed lemmings follow each other in a line; turn around at obstacles; fall off edges; visual animation communicates AI pathfinding and behavioral autonomy
Assigned skills visibly change lemming behavior and appearance (e.g., Climber climbing vertically, Floater deploying parachute); visual feedback reinforces player agency and world mutability
Countdown timer in UI; visual urgency increases as time expires; explosion of all lemmings at zero is ultimate failure state marker

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks in one direction; ignores other lemmings; falls off edges; turns around if hitting obstacle; no skill assignment active
Climbs vertically; falls if hitting overhang; visual climbing animation communicates vertical movement capability
Deploys parachute; descends safely from any height; visual parachute graphic indicates fall-damage immunity
Explodes after 5-second timer; destroys nearby destructible landscape; death animation communicates self-destruct state
Stands still; prevents other lemmings from passing; other lemmings reverse direction on contact; stationary visual state communicates barrier role
Constructs 12-step stairway; stops if head hits object, runs out of bricks, or hits solid object; visible stairway construction animates player agency
Tunnels horizontally/diagonally/vertically; cannot break steel barriers; visual terrain destruction communicates landscape mutability

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Contact with lava, fire, trap, or excessive fall height
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Dies; visual death animation; lemming sprite removed from level; loss state communicated through removal

notes: Lemmings communicates world-state primarily through visual clarity of terrain obstacles, lemming sprite behavior, and real-time environmental feedback. The game lacks faction conflict or political systems—instead, it visualizes player agency through skill assignment mechanics and dynamic landscape modification. Danger and safety are communicated through landscape design (height, hazard placement) and skill-state visuals. Time pressure is the primary narrative tension, visualized through countdown timer. The game's communication layer is fundamentally mechanical: visual feedback (sprite animation, terrain changes, timer) reinforces player understanding of cause-effect relationships between skill assignment and world state change.
